This is a tidy garden, my favorite place, this farm. I must always keep it well. I mean, my life is here. I will put cow dung in it, but I
will make it completely organic. I never use chemicals, and I love this soil very much. I never waste this soil. This is what I will
mainly do. This time, there were 10,000 areca nut saplings, and about 8,000 have been sold. It's a systematic garden. Look at this
garden, a systematic garden where they have done proper planning and plantation. On this side, they have planted a betel nut tree
along with a banana tree, where they have used banana waste to make fertilizer. Today I will show you a garden which is a very
beautiful garden. Look at the beauty of the garden on January 24th, how beautiful it looks. Here you will find long, long bananas,
and in between the bananas, you will find betel nuts. Along with that, you will also find a nursery. In between, they, on that side...
The main cultivation of areca nuts requires money; it will take time. So, if you cultivate bananas, you will need money for the
cultivation. What did you do along with the bananas? You started an areca nut nursery. What happened because of that? If you get
a quick payment, you get money in a single year. Banana production is coming from banana farming. It is being sold from a betel
nut nursery, and the betel nuts have grown big. Look at the height of the betel nuts in two years, a very beautiful height. In two
years, the betel nuts have reached this stage. If you set up a new garden, what will you do? You will do it in this method. And the
person who has done it, brother, let me introduce him to you. I am in Dhekiajuli, and with me is Manoj Kumar Das da. Welcome.
Hello. Let's take a look at the garden. It's a very systematic and tidy garden. Please comment and let me know where you have
seen such a tidy garden. Oh my god, do you always keep it like this? I always keep it like this. Wow, what kind of things have been
done and are still needed? Because this farm is my favorite place. I must always keep it well. I mean, my life is here. Look, I feel
good coming in myself, and if I tell you now, seeing a situation like this has made me feel even more relieved. It's two years old,
just two years since planting the areca nut saplings here. It's been two years since the garden was started. Two years have passed.
In two years, look what you have built in the garden. Please tell us a little about how you did it and what kind of bananas are there.
In this garden, there are 2,500 bananas, 2,000 areca nuts, and also lemons. How many lemons? Three hundred forty. Three
hundred forty lemon plants, and for the small nursery, the saplings were around 10,000 before, sold for 2,000, and now there are
8,000. This time, we will make it one lakh. Everything will be within this. Everything will be betel nut and oil within this. We will
make it one lakh. Wow, look. Because the areca nut saplings will yield money in a single year. It will come in a single year. I mean, I
will do it in black PP for 1 lakh. I will do 1 lakh areca nut saplings. I mean, I will start from April. When will this areca nut be needed?
I am hoping that this production will come after two years. I mean, it has been two years now, and it will come after two years. It
will come, I mean. Even if it's 10%, it will come. Even if it's 10%, it will come after two years. In short, considering my growth, it will
give production in four years. Otherwise, areca nuts take five or six years, and some areca nuts take four years. I mean.

The car has gone to Tezpur for household work. Mother and father will go, and the driver will go with them. But let's show you what
all they have taken in the car while going. This is Sanchipuli, this is Sanchipuli. This will go to Jorhat. Sanchipuli is on this side; this
much will go to Sanchipuli. There are seedlings on this side towards Tezpur; these seedlings will go to Gauri Sagar. Meaning, if you
fall on the same route, there are no seedlings here. I am showing you what is here. Let me just fill this up, wait. I have filled this up
on this side, the seedlings. Go that way in the car; I am showing you what is here. Since mother and father have a two-day... They
have a get-together to attend a function. Friends will meet together in Tezpur. Since they are father's friends, the mother and father
will go. And on this side, you can see their belongings. On this side are their bags. As you can see, there are three bags each, and
on this side, there is one plate of eggs. They will deliver this egg plate to Jorhat. One person needs it to incubate their eggs, so this
is the egg for that. This is the pickle, the pickle they take from us and use for business. This pickle will now go to Dergaon. Again,
when it falls on the road, they will come out and take it from there. And show it this way. It seems there are Bhut Jolokia half kg,
Amara half kg, and Outenga half kg. Someone in Tezpur is also looking for this pickle, the pickle which... They have taken it from us
for testing. In the next phase, they will take it commercially so that they can create a business again. The thing is, you have to run
your businesses very well with the things you have, meaning without spending any extra money. Normally, we provide such a 'puli'
or 'puli' service. We provide it on the bus, we provide it by 'super.' For example, to Guwahati or, let's say, to Tinsukia, we keep
sending it by 'super.' But when such facilities are available, we send it in our own vehicle because our fare cost is reduced. Now, if
we take a carton, such a carton... If we send such cartons by bus, it costs around 100 rupees. This one has about 30 pieces. This
carton has about 100 pieces. They charge 200 rupees for this carton. This means if we send an average carton by bus, they charge
150 to 200 rupees, where a person's money is lost, and we also have to bear a small loss. But when such an opportunity arises,
what happens is that we can send the goods in our own car. This is what you see with the pickles. There are about 700 packets of
pickles here, which they have taken from us. They have taken non-branded pickles, and then they will label and sell their own
stickers. This means it will be a business for them. They have taken the pickles from us for 12 rupees, and when they retail them,
they can charge 20 rupees, or if they wholesale them, they can charge 16 rupees. This means they get a profit from one packet of
pickles. They are making a profit of about four rupees in wholesale. The half-kg pickle packets that you saw, they have taken those
as samples. We have also sent the samples, and they will leave them on the road. They will have another business where they can
take their business from us without any practice. In the case of the Sanchi saplings, let me show you. You can see these Sanchi
saplings up close. These Sanchi saplings are very beautiful. These Sanchi saplings are this year's Sanchi saplings. You can see them
here; they are almost... The seedlings will be one inch, one foot. I told you that these seedlings will go to Jorhat. The seedlings you
see on this side are a bit taller. These are seedlings that are about two feet tall. The seedlings will go to Tezpur, and these will go...
Again, to your Gaurisagar, meaning the Gaurisagar of Sivasagar. You will find it on the way. They will come out, I will make a phone
call, and they will come and take it. The main thing is... Connections, how you can make the connections, how much effort you can
put in. Now, coming at 7 AM, I have loaded the things like this and kept them ready for tomorrow. Now, after loading the things, I
will go by car. You have seen in my previous videos, and have seen before as well, that... In chicken trading, one can earn four to
five thousand rupees a day. Just buy chickens from others and sell them. So, if we keep doing these things, money will keep coming
in side by side, and your business will continue to run. And it's not enough to be ashamed of your own products. Come this way.
Why am I saying it's not okay to be shy? You see this place, the farm area, right here I've put up a banner. Why? Because this is our
nursery. We sell saplings here, so why be shy? It's a nursery for life and livelihood. I have planted it, and on this side, we have
stocked all the seedlings here. We do the main stocking here, and if you look, you will see seedlings everywhere. Let me show you
from the inside. Look at the seedlings on this side. Here you have different types of seedlings. From this side, different, different,
different, different, different seedlings. Again, different ones came from this side, and different ones came from that side. On this
side, our Golaghat's oilseed seedlings have arrived. Again, on that side, we emptied that shed and stocked the seedlings there.
Now, if you talk about seedling stock, we have about 18,000 seedling stocks here, in the entire nursery. On this side, it's about the
seedling stock again. Let me show you. On this side, there are seedlings. On this side, some high-height seedlings. On this side,
some high-height seedlings. These seedlings in the back are even taller. The one we have is a 2.5-foot-sized seedling. On this side,
there are sandalwood saplings. On this side, there are mahogany. You saw the teak saplings and fruit saplings earlier. We brought
them here again and stocked the seedlings. You can see here, we have almost all the seedlings here. Around four and a half
thousand seedlings. When we filled this bed, about three thousand seedlings were placed, and in this bed, about fifteen hundred
seedlings were placed. This is the stock of seedling saplings. And I will show you two things about the size of the seedling's stem. I
will show you the difference in the stem's size. This time, the number of stalks that have come, meaning the size and previous size
of the seedlings we brought this time, we will show you. You will understand when you come this way. Look here, you can see this is
the previous stock we had. This is the size of the seedling stalks. Look at this. And this time, the number of seedlings we have
brought... Look at the size of its stem. You will see a big difference between the two. You see this, the size of this stem is bigger,
meaning the sapling in this stem will be even bigger, the sapling which...

your businesses very well with the things you have, meaning without spending any extra money. Normally, we provide such a 'puli'

150 to 200 rupees, where a person's money is lost, and we also have to bear a small loss. But when such an opportunity arises,
what happens is that we can send the goods in our own car. This is what you see with the pickles. There are about 700 packets of

pickles. They are making a profit of about four rupees in wholesale. The half-kg pickle packets that you saw, they have taken those
as samples. We have also sent the samples, and they will leave them on the road. They will have another business where they can
take their business from us without any practice. In the case of the Sanchi saplings, let me show you. You can see these Sanchi

in side by side, and your business will continue to run. And it's not enough to be ashamed of your own products. Come this way.

Have you seen flowers blooming on the areca nut tree? Today, I am in the house of a person who also has flowers blooming on the

eats them. Look up here at the areca nut tree. There are different trees, and flowers have bloomed on them. In March-April, they
have over a thousand Kopou flowers and various other types of orchids. He has about four hundred types of orchids in his own
garden. This man does not do commercial orchid business. To preserve all these orchids, he brings them from our inner jungle and
forest and plants them in his own house's areca nut trees. This is a unique work within Assam, something we do within Assam. It's a
task that deserves a salute because if this work is done, many won't be able to do it, and a commercial aspect will come. Today, I
will introduce that person. Come, uncle, I am in Chirang district, and with me is, come forward, Uncle Anshuram Narzary. I welcome
you. First, tell me your location. Our district is Chirang district, and Bengtol region, and Ontheibari village. I have given his contact
number on the screen because when you come, you will be able to see various types of universities and colleges. People come to
him to do research on orchids. He is a very busy person; he always goes to the forest and brings orchids. He brings them and plants
them in his house, on the trees. You will see orchids all around the house. I never thought I would meet a person like this after
coming to Chirang. Today is January 26th. If we come next... If you come in March-April, you will see complete flowers blooming
here. Last year, it was full of flowers, and this year too, it will be full of flowers. So, you can come if you are visiting the next plant in
March-April. Let's start the video and see the flowers, and from the Orchid Man of Assam, we know, from the Orchid Man of Assam,
we know how he will work with orchids, how orchids... why is he an orchid lover? Let's understand everything that has been done.
Let's start the video. I have been associated with orchids for a long time. Now I have over three hundred orchids like this. All of
them are well-grown orchids. That means wild orchids. Along with that, I also have some hybrid ones, which are called
'bonosongkor'. I have many of these too. Okay, when you planted these orchids, I saw that they are completely natural. You see the
entire orchid on this areca nut tree? On this side, there is an areca nut tree, an areca nut tree. We already knew that orchids are
found in the forest, on the areca nut tree. Now you have planted orchids all over this entire tree. How did you plant them, or from
where did you collect the orchids? For example, on our national highway, there were orchids from Karigar to Bhutan. All of them
were forests, but they have cut down all these trees and planted them here. They planted everything. What is the planting method?
I mean, they haven't been able to bring all the seeds; they have only brought as much as they could. People have destroyed them.
And when people come here, they sit here. Yes, they sit here comfortably. And really, look at the feeling, you can sit under this
areca nut tree in a very peaceful environment and watch the orchids and flowers. To see the flowers, you have to go to the marsh.
This is what we call a 'kopou'. Yes, this is the 'kopou' flower. Okay, and this one below is a 'nobil', a dendrobium 'nobil'. These buds
are coming out, these small ones. After all the leaves fall, all the flowers will come again. All the flowers will come. And what is
there? Tell me little by little, and how did you plant these on top? Dendrobium fimbritum. This is called Pimritam.

title: ?1.49 Lakh Electric Superbike ? Ultraviolette X-47
description:
People often say Electric bikes are expensive… but that’s changing. ?

The Ultraviolette X-47 now starts at just ?1.49 lakh with the UV Battery Flex model.  

This also covers battery maintenance, repairs & replacement, plus long-term warranty support.

Visit the UV Space Station to know more.
??Shakti Tower, Mahavir Nagar, Patna

#ultraviolette #electricbike #evindia
insights:

- Key feature themes detected: Engine Performance, Mileage, Durability and Body, Price and Offer, Service and Maintenance.

page_content:
People say that EV bikes are expensive, but not anymore. Wait, an electric superbike can start from ₹1.49 lakh. Yes, this is
Ultraviolet X47. It seems impossible, right? Because normally, EV bikes' battery is almost half the price of the bike's price. But here
is an interesting part: this bike comes with battery as a service. Meaning, you buy the bike's chassis and use the battery as a
subscription, like you use Netflix and Spotify by subscribing. Meaning, simply, you pay the same amount for your bike as you ride
on your battery subscription. Petrol bikes generally cost ₹4 to ₹5 per kilometer, and engine servicing and maintenance add to the
cost. But in the X47 battery flex model, the starting price is just ₹1.5 lakhs, that is ₹1.49 lakhs, and the running cost is roughly ₹2 to
₹3 per kilometer. And the battery maintenance is also covered. With the Ultraviolet X47, the more you ride, the more you save.

page_content:
Farmer brothers, today Sonalika has launched its new tractor series, the Gold series, and in this, two new tractors have been
launched, brother, DI 55 Third and DI 745 Third. We will get complete information about them, but first, let me show you a glimpse
of the event. After this, we will take you to the plant. Inside the plant, brother, every 2 minutes, Sonalika's new tractor comes out.
So, I will show you a little bit of how tractors are prepared inside the plant, in terms of minutes. 145 DI Gold Series. So, let's go to
Sonalika's plant where tractors come out in minutes. So, you can see here, brother, this is Sonalika's ITL plant in Hoshiarpur,
Punjab, and here, how are tractors prepared? How do tractors come out after being made in minutes? How much complex work
goes on behind it? How much effort is put in, you can see it here. Work is also done inside the plant by machines, by labor, and by
robots. So here, one by one, parts are joined separately, and parts are made inside this plant, and these parts are assembled here,
and all these parts keep joining, and the tractor keeps getting made. And here, in the end, the tractor takes its final form, brother.
So, as you mentioned, this is our new 55 three-cylinder Gold series product, which has been upgraded in Gold styling. First, what we
see in the tractor is the bumper. It comes factory-fitted; you don't have to make additional payments for it, it comes with the
company. And in this, you can see its weight has been increased. It has been made even heavier. This bumper is 90 kg, and along
with that, you have been given a provision to install three 18.5 kg plates as well. And along with that, it comes with very impressive
branding of DI 55. Also, the branding of our Sonalika, the Gold series branding, is given on the gold badging so that it stands out.
Also, a signature decal has been given for the 30 years of our Sonalika company. Now, coming to the main part, which is our
engine. This tractor comes with a powerful 3-cylinder engine of 3532 cc, which has a 107 bore and a stroke length of 131. This
stroke length of 131, because of this, our tractor also generates a powerful torque of 235 Newton meters, which we also provide in
writing. Absolutely. Now, this engine of ours is the largest engine in the Indian tractor industry. No tractor manufacturer has a larger
engine than this in a three-cylinder. Yes. Moving forward, it gives you a 65-liter fuel tank, which enables you to work for long
intervals. Yes. And now, if we talk about clutch housing, in this, a double clutch is available, hand clutch, IPTO as we call it, that
provision is given, which allows your transmission to move with your feet and the PTO clutch with your hands. This also makes your
clutch life longer. Okay. Now, if we talk about the platform, we have a small, these are all features, but it has a lot of effect. The
farmer used to have to climb by holding the steering wheel first. But now, a grab rail is given; you can climb easily with it. It comes
with a fully flat platform. Our Ergo Deck platform is this, in which you get a speed of approximately 34.5 kilometers per hour. We

Carrots can yield a net profit of ₹1 to ₹3 lakh, but the condition is that you should have a little information. You will get absolutely
red carrots; take a look. The color is the same from the outside as it is from the inside. What grade of carrots is this coming out of?
This is of grade number one, brother. So, these carrots, after being washed, are filled into sacks like this. If you talk about ₹20 per
kg, then the average will be ₹2.5-3 lakh per kg. Farmers, if you get the right information about carrots and the right seeds, then its
profit can be double or triple that of wheat and mustard. Pawan Bhai is currently standing in his field in the Sonipat district of
Haryana. He has planted carrots on 30-35 acres and has been planting carrots for 17-18 years. So, he has a lot of good experience
with carrot farming. He is a big farmer in this area. So, we will get all the A to Z information from him. Carrot seeds matter a lot. It is
very important to choose the variety. Even today, Pawan Bhai, when we talk to him, the yield is halved. So, let's talk to Pawan Bhai;
he has quite good information about carrots. 
Pawan Bhai, welcome to Dear Kisan. Hello. So, first of all, tell us in how many acres you have cultivated and in how many acres you
have planted carrots. Actually, there are about 100 acres, but you have planted carrots in 35 acres. Out of 100, carrots have been
planted in 35 acres. And how long have you been planting carrots? It's been 17-18 years. You have been continuously planting
carrots for 17-18 years. Yes. So, what can we consider the right time to plant carrots? Actually, we might start installing it in August,
but it depends on the rain. It might start in August-September. We can plant it from August to September. We can plant it in
October as well. We can plant it in October as well. The rest depends on the weather. It depends on the weather. Okay. So, the
thing we are trying to learn from you is how to choose the right seeds, how to increase production, how will carrots be tasty?
Because it will have taste, color, and size. So, production will increase, and the rate will be good. And the farmer plants for the rate,
plants for production, so that he can get a good profit from other crops. So, tell me a little bit from the beginning how you sow; after
that, we will talk about the seeds. 
See, about sowing, the main thing is to plow the field. The more you plow, the drier the field should be. That will understand the
good field; it will understand automatically. It depends on the seed. Then we will sow hybrid; we will sow desi. Compared to desi,
hybrid gives double profit. It gives double the profit. If we talk about production, it yields double the production compared to the
local variety. It doubles. These local carrots, some turn out small, some turn out big, some are thick, some are thin. So, the yield
doesn't become average. But all the hybrid carrots that are standing here, you'll find them all of the same size, long ones; there's
no difference in that. Absolutely, Pawan Bhai, if we talk about hybrid, it will be the same size and the same quality throughout the
field. 
First, I would like to know a little from you about the time of sowing, what kind of fertilizer you put in it. Please tell me a little about
that. At the time of sowing, we put a cut DAP in it, potash, and zinc sulfur, etc. But this is not compulsory either; you can put it if
you want; otherwise, it is not necessary. It depends on the soil of your field, which fertilizer is lacking; that should be put more. Yes,
in the field where we are sowing carrots for a long time, sowing again and again, then there is a need to put it in, whether it is the
first time carrots have been sown or it has been two to four years, there is not much need for it. As far as I can see, you have
planted carrots on the bed. So, is it necessary to make beds, or can they be planted anyway? Carrots are sown from the bed itself,
from the machine itself, in today's time. Earlier, that used to happen; that system is gone now. Almost all the machines in the area
have become carrot sowing ones. Absolutely. So, carrots are now being sown on the bed. And Pawan Bhai is producing twice or
thrice the production compared to before, taking the support of technology. Yes. Absolutely, and we are trying to learn the same
things from Pawan Bhai. 
So, he mentioned about sowing, Pawan Bhai also about variety. So, you mentioned about the variety; you have been planting
carrots for 17-18 years; you farm on a large area. Which varieties did you plant before, and which varieties are you planting now?
You have planted indigenous varieties, and you have also planted hybrids. So, what has been your experience with seeds, what
advice would you like to give to the farmers? See, for the past several years, I used to sow indigenous varieties of Sengro. But in the
end, the same thing used to happen; our carrots would stop selling. So, some carrots were thick, some thin, some small, some big;
a lot of problems would arise. The demand for raw carrots was high; our carrots would stop selling at that time, the carrots in our
area. The carrots that started being sown in the new area, those carrots were good. It started yielding produce and increased in
terms of color and shine. Then we started hybrid. We are from them. They are selling carrots quite well compared to them since
they started growing hybrid varieties. The market is so slow, but the yield is so high that even with a little labor, we can get more
work done, and our carrots will become average. And if the rate is right, there is no expectation that it will be ₹1 lakh, ₹1.5 lakh, ₹2
lakh, or ₹3 lakh, or even more than that. Carrots can make a net profit of ₹1 to ₹3 lakh, but the condition is that you should have a
little information. You will have to plant by taking the help of technology and selecting the right seeds. 
You mentioned that you have planted a hybrid variety, so there are different varieties in hybrid as well; there are many varieties in
the market; the farmer gets confused about which one to plant. So, first of all, carrot harvesting is going on at this time; the farmer
is taking out the carrots and taking them to the market. So, you should go and see in that farmer's field. How is it standing, what
variety was it? So, it will be clear from there. Like Pawan Bhai has planted hybrid here. So, which one have you planted in hybrid?
Manna Bhaiya, these are planted Myco 404 number. Okay. If we talk about its specialty, then every year we plant one, two, or
another carrot. But we don't think that any other carrot has been able to survive equally compared to this carrot. It doesn't survive.
Look at this; it has been about five months since the field was planted, and the field hasn't developed a flag yet. Absolutely, there is
no flag, brother. And the carrots are tasty to eat, and if someone says they want them to turn green or black, there is no complaint
with these carrots, brother. Absolutely. If you uproot the carrots, you'll get one size. They will be thick according to the time.
Anyway, even today if you don't eat carrots, if you pluck them, you'll get completely red carrots from the inside, you'll see. It
couldn't even be ground in the carrot. Absolutely, brother, what they say is that each person doesn't need proof, so Pawan Bhai
broke the carrot here and showed it, and you see this, brother. The color is the same as it is from the outside; see, it's red from the
inside too. The stem of it, Pawan Bhai, is also red. It's completely red. Absolutely, and because they have directly uprooted it from
the field, if we wash it, it will become completely bright, and its color will come out. Yes. When it runs in the machine, the shine is
also much more in the carrot. Hmm hmm. Absolutely. So, you take the carrot out of here and take it to the machine for washing,

and then for packing. Yes. Farmer brothers bring carrots here after studying in the field. Let me show you once; look, this is a carrot
dairy. They directly uproot from the field and bring it here, then through this machine. After washing the carrots, they are taken out
in different grades and packed here and taken to the market. So, let's get Pawan Bhai to check the quality, etc., once. 
Pawan Bhai, which grade of carrot is this coming out of? This is of grade one, brother. It is of A grade. This is of A grade. A grade
carrot, the micro variety you have put in hybrid, what percentage does it come out of, and what percentage does it come out of in
desi? This will come out 80%. 80% will come out; some will come out thick, approximately. In that, many times half of it comes out,
more waste comes out, the field is breaking apart. Now, in this, you don't find carrots to see broken pieces. Very few, if we are
putting 200-250 sacks, those eight-10 sacks have come out, the rest are all A grade; some are good, some are bad, we are calling it
one number. One number is there; some are thick, some are in number two. The new farmers who are watching this video, who
don't have information about carrot farming, so show them once that this is A grade carrot, this is B grade carrot, there is junk lying
below. So show that once. Look, this carrot. So, its rate also seems good. So, in this way, carrots come out of the farmer's field, and
in this way, after being cleaned in the machine, they go inside the market, packed. So, when the vegetables are taken to the
market, prices are fixed after looking at the quality, etc. The vegetables with good quality sell quickly. Have you ever felt that the
hybrid carrots you have planted are Myco's 404? You must have noticed its demand is higher compared to the local ones. The
demand has automatically been higher. It has more color, shine, length, and width; everything is more than that. And if we talk
about the rate, then compared to this, it sells a lot more in comparison to the local variety. Let's say it sells for one or two rupees,
say for a thousand rupees, or even twelve hundred. There is always a difference of two hundred rupees per sack for us. The rate is
higher according to the quality. Yes, and many times what happens is, many times the rate in the market has dropped a lot. Even at
that time, this carrot will only be harvested; it will be dug from the field; it will go; the market will be sold; it will give an average;
then some profit will come. Absolutely. Compared to desi, but desi, if they plant it many times, then in that... If carrots are light and
the yield is not too low in the market, and there is a difference in sales, then we often have to plow the field, and carrots do not
reach the market. Absolutely, as Pawan Bhai mentioned, there is another point here, Pawan Bhai. As you mentioned, the rate goes
down, the rate of vegetables remains up and down; it never remains stable. If the rates go down and you have planted native
carrots. So, he will remove the flag. He will remove the flag, and if we talk about labor, the biggest problem with carrots and onions
is that the labor cost is high. And to cover the labor cost, in the market... The market price is whatever it may be. The labor cost will
remain the same. But when there is more, then the production of less labor is able to reach there. So, compared to desi, it should
be available in this. That's the main point. By deducting expenses, something should be given, so that the field's carrots can be
dug. Absolutely. This much is needed, that we have to plow the field. So, these are the things inside carrots. 
Pawan Bhai, what approximate rate should we consider? For example, if carrots are sold for ₹10 per kilo, then there is a profit. If
sold for ₹5 per kilo, then there is a loss. Is there any such? There will be no profit; we will remain almost equal. If it's desi, if it sells
for ₹10 per kg, we will incur a loss. We will remain equal in this. If we get ₹10, ₹12, ₹15 more than this, then it will increase in
quantity. If you talk about ₹20 per kg, then per kg... It will become an average of ₹2.5-3 lakh. Absolutely. So, they also mentioned a
special point here that compared to desi seeds, there is also a difference in hybrid that if the rate is low, then its production goes
out more from the field. So, it gets covered a bit. Now, not much of a market is running, a market of ₹10-12 per kg is running, but
the fields that are looking good are still giving ₹1.5-2 lakh. Absolutely, because the quality and quantity are higher there. Yes, the
quantity is higher. Even at a low rate, they are still giving good profit to the farmer. So, this is the difference, brother; you should
leave the desi seeds of carrots and shift to hybrid. And as I tell you every time, the things you are planting, the technique you are
using in your field, if you see something new, you see a new seed, you see a new variety, then as a demo, we can try planting it in
one or two acres. You can do it, I mean, I would like to say that the landlords who have planted desi seeds, instead of doing more,
try planting in a few acres; they will automatically get hybrid seeds, that's the guarantee. Absolutely, this is a guarantee, brother.
The farmer who plants it once will know because its production is approximately one and a half to two and a half times more than
the local one. We can consider it almost double. We can consider it double. 
Pawan Bhai has a good experience. He has cultivated carrots in 30-35 acres. Currently, their harvesting has been done here. The
carrots that you saw being washed in the video clips, he has started sending all of them to the market, brother. He mentioned the
rates, etc. Currently, they are making a decent profit. The rest have shifted to hybrid. So, if you also plant desi seeds of carrots, try
hybrid once. In which, the 404 number variety of Myco is in Pawan Bhai's field.

condition, as you can see in front of you. So, what is the current update of all 17 varieties? I will tell you that, brother. I have written
all these things down. I will tell you what things are written in it. See, the variety name on this side is 1402 2851, and where I
sprayed PGR, the weed control medicine, its record is separate, and where I didn't spray, inside the 'adi,' its record is separate here,
like it's 1402, written twice. PGR and without PGR, similarly 2851 PBW 872 826 711. And on this side are all the varieties. So, I have
noted their double record, where the PGR spray was done, that's separate, and where it wasn't done, that's separate. See, there is a
difference in height between day and night. For example, if we look at 872, the PGR spray was done, where the height went to 103
centimeters; where it wasn't done, it went to 113 centimeters. In 826, there is also a height of 104 centimeters, where the PGR
spray was done; where it wasn't done, it is 116 centimeters. So, where the PGR spray was done, the height maintained. What is the
length of the ears? Which variety is it? The size of everyone's ears is different. Look, some have long ears, some have short ears.
What is the probable number of grains inside it? So, look, these are also different. So, I have noted down this thing of all the
varieties. So, these are half varieties on this side, which is the front of this side. The front of half the varieties is on that side, so I
will show you that once. This is the other side, and here is WH 1105. After this, here is 2967, and after this, here is HD 3386, this
variety. After this, here is 372. Look at its hairs. And DBW 222, DBW 303, and this is WH 1270. So, all the varieties are placed here,
and with good management, with good management, these varieties are placed here so that we can collect all their data here and
tell you that, brother. This variety and all the things are put in the same way, whether it's fertilizer, water, all are given in the same

tell you all the things in detail, and this was a desi variety. Look at this. This has fallen down. This is Sona Moti, which has round
grains. Look at its ears; they are completely different from all others. And one more thing I'll show you, look at its ears, brother.
These are its longest ears, the G13. Here, let me show you a little bit. Look at this. It has ears of 24 centimeters, 25 centimeters in
length. And here also you can clearly see. Look, brother, how long the ears are, okay? So here, not one, all of its ears are of the

all these 17 varieties. First, a complete video will come, in which I will show you roughly everything. After that, I will make separate
videos of these varieties. You will get 17 different videos of 17. In them, I will tell you the details of each variety in detail. So all
things will become clear, brother. It's a world of difference. Look at this, 2851, 1402. So different characters have emerged from all
the varieties, and currently, we are seeing different results from them. So in this video, I had to give you this one piece of

page_content:
Hello, farmer friends, welcome to today's new exciting video. Friends, Mahindra has a launch event, and inside this launch event,
you can see that Mahindra has launched a new huge range here. As you can see, Mahindra 585 DI, you are seeing these tractors
with a completely latest look, and within this, you can also get some customization done. The company has now provided better
torque than before in this tractor. The company has now enhanced the features in this tractor compared to before. We will provide
complete information. Hello, farmer friends, I am your friend Indrajeet Patel, and you are watching the Real Agriculture World
YouTube channel. So, if you are watching the video for the first time, please like, follow, and subscribe to the channel because we
keep bringing similar videos for you.
First of all, the biggest change, friends, has been made in the cosmetic changes of this tractor. The cosmetic change is quite
significant. The entire bonnet of the tractor has been changed. It has an air cleaner, a three-stage oil bath pre-type air cleaner
installed inside this tractor. If we talk about the silencer, it is the same as the saddle-type silencer you used to see before; it is of
the same type. If we talk about the front axle, you will find a heavy-duty non-adjustable company-fitted front axle inside this
tractor. And if we talk about the power steering, the company has provided you with dual-acting hydraulic balance-type power
steering inside this tractor, which will provide you with the facility to turn in the least amount of space.
Along with that, farmer friends, there have been even more new changes inside this tractor. If we talk about the tires, you can now
see the front tire. You get to see a 7.50-16 front tire inside this tractor, and if we talk about the back tire, the company has provided
a 14.9-28 tire here. If we talk about stickers and badging, then here, as you can see, Narmadehar. You can register this thing in
your name as well, meaning you can get the stickering done. If a farmer wants customization within the sticker, then the farmer can
get their name written here. You can get the name of any deity, etc., written according to your choice. You get a single step bar
here to see. A single footstep is given. And along with that, you can see the badging, etc. You will get a dual clutch, CRP due, okay?
And many new options have been added. There is a significant change in the seating arrangement, and I find this tractor more
comfortable to sit in than before. It feels comfortable, meaning the company has done next-level work on comfort as well inside this
tractor.
Also, friends, if I talk about the dashboard, etc., inside this tractor, the company has completely changed the dashboard of this
tractor. You can see that the dashboard of this tractor has undergone a total change. The complete changes have been made here.
The company has also provided a digitally typed instrument cluster. You get to see an analog RPM meter. The steering wheel is
quite good, and here you are given a hand accelerator with very smooth shifting. The controlling unit for the horn lights has now
been shifted here. You are going to get better features than before. Here, you have been given a brake lever, a hand brake lever.
Meaning, the company has included features like a car inside this tractor. Add-ons have been added. You will find the low-high lever
here. If we talk about the right side of the driver, you will find a main four-gear. You will find the reverse gear. If we talk about
brakes, etc., you are going to get oil-immersed multi-plated brakes inside this tractor with smooth shift. And if we talk about the
clutch, etc., it has a dual clutch. With half apply, you can operate the tractor's transmission, and with full apply, you can operate the
tractor's PDO.
Also, friends, if we talk about it here, then to store tools, etc., the toolbox has been shifted here; earlier, it used to come there. And
during the rainy season, when you used to park the tractor, there were often problems with the clutch sticking, etc. So, the
company has added another feature here. You can see it. The clutch, during the rainy season, when you park the tractor for a long
time, you can adjust the clutch of this tractor from here. When you park it for a long time, you can lock it this way and park it. And
you can keep it in a lock-unlock position as you wish. The gap of the fender, etc., has also been greatly enhanced because this
tractor has now become purlin special. With this tractor, you can use multifunctional agricultural implements, etc., and one more
thing has been changed quite well. Here, the grab handle you are seeing, its supporting bar has been made quite heavy, and along
with that, the sitting arrangements have also been improved. Along with that, farmer friends, here you get to see the changes
towards the front, like cosmetic changes, etc. And here's one more thing, the tires that used to come before, you have a 14.9-28
rear tire. This thing is quite good. MRF company tires, etc., are given. If we talk about the indicators, etc., you get to see a change.
The brake light and indicator have been customized a bit.
Mahalift, meaning farmer friends, the lifting capacity you used to see inside this tractor was around 1600-1700 kg. Now the
company has, inside this tractor, given you a better lifting capacity of 2000 kilograms, and from here, the chain has been removed,
and step bars have been installed with a multifunctional point. Also, friends, Mahindra has made a big upgrade inside this tractor on
the sensing of this tractor. Like you used to get to see single points here before, and there were a lot of complaints coming from our
farmer brothers that these things should be improved. So, the company, this time, did not leave you any space to put your hand. So
many features have been upgraded inside this tractor. Here you get three top linkage points, fully automatically with deft and draft
control bar. If I talk about the distributor valve, you will get heavy-duty company-fitted double distributor valves, and along with
that, if we talk about the PTU horsepower, then with 33.5 kilowatts, you get a PTU power of 44.9 horsepower inside this tractor. It
has been given. Also, if we talk about operating the distributor valve, when you trolley up-dump, then here you are given the
engagement of the lever. Here, you also get a jack on the right side, like when you use a multifunctional implement, you can use
those things. Mahindra has finally improved the backlight this time as well. They have increased the small light and installed a
bigger one. I liked this thing quite a bit.
Otherwise, friends, if we talk about it here, then you side shift. You get to see a two-speed PDU, 540 standard, and in gears, the
company has now added PDU inside this tractor. Completely, a lot has changed inside the tractor, of which we have provided
information in this video. Let me show you a little bit. This was a tractor. Now we will show you another model of Mahindra, the
Mahindra 585 DI that I showed. It is standing here. Mahindra 585 DI, you will find many gear options. In tractors like that, the gear
options were less. In these tractors, if we talk about gear options, you will find more of them. Meaning, you can take more gear, etc.
In this tractor, there are 8 forward, 2 reverse, 8 forward, 8 reverse gears. You will also find options, and behind you, you are seeing
a tractor parked there, which is Mahindra's talking tractor. Meaning, a tractor, farmer brothers, that talks to you, okay? And in

Mahindra's new series, a new tractor, a new model, Mahindra 585 75 DI. This is a Mahindra 575 DI, and if we talk about this tractor,
like we just showed you the Mahindra 585. That was our 50 HP tractor. If we talk about this, then this is a 47 HP tractor.
So, farmer friends, this was Mahindra's new 2026 launch event. How did you like the new Mahindra 585 tractor and the Mahindra
575? Be sure to comment and let us know. More upcoming videos are coming very soon, including Mahindra Talking Tractors and
such. So, share the video experience with us, like the video, and subscribe to the channel. I was your friend Indrajit Patel, and you
were watching Real Agriculture World. Jai Jawan, Jai Kisan.

one bike, how great is that? So, I'll tell you five reasons why you should buy the TVS Raider 125. I have the top variant of the TVS

automatically turn off in four to five seconds. Then, by placing the clutch, you can start the bike and drive the bike, under which you
also get the feature of silent start, in which there is no sound of the alternator and starter motor. The bike starts smoothly. Now,
after these features, if I talk further, then in the bike, you also get GTT, Glide Through Traffic, in which when you drive the bike in
traffic, just by releasing a slight clutch, you can drive at a speed of four to five without an accelerator. By the way, those things are
available. If you check its instrument console, you also get voice assist in Bluetooth, which allows you to do some tasks. Basically,
we can also talk about how it will do the work you want it to do, according to its limitations. It has 99+ features, meaning it will do a
lot of work. In this, when you connect Smart X Connect, you will also see a lot of connectivity related to turn-by-turn navigation on
its instrument console. And its instrument console is quite good and visible, so you can comfortably see it in any sunlight, like it's
sunny right now, it's so bright. You can easily see it in this as well, so there's no problem. So, you saw that in the bike, if we check
the reasons first, then at the ex-showroom price of just ₹96,000, you get single-channel ABS and lots of features in this. If you take
the base variant in which you don't need features, then you will get this bike for just ₹82,000, that is, at the ex-showroom price of
₹82,000. Abhimit TVS Patna, Rajendra Nagar, you can go and take its test ride. All the details are given in the description. After the
video, please do go and take a test ride. If we talk about reason number two, its compact design makes it only 123 kg, which, if you
fill fuel, will go up to approximately 130 kg. Plus, to drive it comfortably on the road in traffic, you also get a ground clearance of
180 mm, which, even if you sit, even if you have more weight, you will get a minimum of 170. So, you get that much ground
clearance. Let me tell you one more thing in this, which is good for local use. The bike's wheelbase is quite like a sport bike. So,
whenever you ride the bike, you can take U-turns, turns at short distances, even in this bike, by tilting the bike to one side, you can
even do a 360 in one place. So, this bike provides the facility for all these things. Plus, because it has a power mode, it becomes a
little easier to do this work there, so you will get that here. It gets support for a split seat with wider seats, which have a better
width. So, when you check the bike or when you sit on this bike, you will definitely get a pretty good feel. You don't get sliding seats
or smooth seats like in a normal commuter bike, which move you forward. You won't get that. If you check the back seat as well,
you will find a storage space here where you can keep something.

Hello, farmer brothers. Farmer brothers, don't you also get laborers on time for harvesting your crops? So, in today's video, I have
brought for you a tractor-mounted ripper binder machine, using which you can not only harvest your crops but also tie bundles to
them. So, let's start today's video and learn about this Greenland Company's tractor-mounted ripper binder machine. Farmer
brothers, the tractor-mounted ripper binder machine that you are seeing comes from Greenland Company, and if you harvest your
crops with this, then this machine runs at a width of 7 feet and harvests your crops in 7 feet. Along with this, if we talk about at
what height from the ground it harvests its crops, then here you will see that its cutter bar is only 3 centimeters to 5 centimeters in
height from the ground; it harvests your crops in that. That is, your crop is going to remain below just by name, and you can
harvest your crops with it with great cleanliness. They attach it to that tractor, and it has a PTO system. So, from here, you can...
The PTO of the tractor, you connect it to the PTO of your tractor and harvest your crops. When you harvest your crops, along with
harvesting the crop, there is a ripper here, and its binder is attached here. Along with harvesting the crops, it also ties the crops
here. For this, a separate plastic thread is used, a box is attached to it, in which you can tie your crops with that thread along with
harvesting. This saves a lot of your effort. Only in the ripper machine that you take, only that also depends on how expert your
operator is, how much experience he has, how long he has been harvesting his crops, and how long he has been using the machine.
It depends on that. You can harvest your crop from one to one and a half acres per hour. So, in this way, farmer brothers, you can
operate this machine. Along with this, farmer brothers, many farmers need that we don't want this reaper binder; we want a reaper
like this, a 7-foot one, but we don't want a tying system because we often have soybean crops that also you can harvest your crops
from a height of 3 centimeters to 5 centimeters of land. This is a semi-variant. The only difference between the two is that the
reaper we told you about is a reaper binder, and this is just a reaper. With this, you will only harvest your crops. You harvest the
crops and put them on one side, and this is a reaper binder. With this, you will be able to harvest your crops and also tie them along
with harvesting. So, these are both things. From these two, according to your convenience, you can see what you need from this.
Also, on this machine, you also get a subsidy from the government. The subsidy for this varies from state to state. For example, in
our Rajasthan, the ripper machines have a subsidy of up to 50%. All the different states may have different subsidies, but this
machine from the company is a government-approved machine, so if you are getting a subsidy in your state, you will get the
subsidy. You can check on the government's website, the Agriculture Department's, to see how much subsidy is available there.
Along with this, farmer brothers, if you want to buy this machine, which dealers are available near you in which city? You can visit
that dealer and see the machine and purchase this machine. So, farmer brothers, I hope you liked our video. If you liked this video,
then like this video and also share this video with other farmer brothers. I will meet you in the next video with another interesting
piece of information. Till then, thank you.

get the rate. Mainly, farmers who cultivate vegetables, please remember. Those who want to cultivate vegetables continuously
should definitely ensure that vegetables are available in their garden, whether it is any season, any time, whether it is the rainy
season, whether it is the cold season, or whether it is the summer season. Because they don't know when the rate will come, those
who cultivate vegetables should definitely ensure that vegetables are available in their garden for the maximum ever. Then, if the
rate decreases once in a while, it will be a profitable opportunity for us. Another thing is that many farmers, when they start new,
when we go to the field and see, you need to know what is going out in the market and at what time and where it is going. After
knowing, don't incur losses by planting just 2, 3, or 4 acres. Invest in large quantities, so know the market. Which variety is going to
climb, whether it's okra, eggplant, or tomato, which variety to plant, which size, which color is going to climb, learn and plant
accordingly. Don't lose by planting in large quantities in acres. There are many people who have lost like this. Because we started
the field just yesterday or the day before, this is the problem they mention when they go there. So, farmers, remember this one
thing. Mainly, don't do it in such a way that big markets are run by big farmers. Plant three or four types in one acre. If you plant
like that, there is a chance that one type will not get a good rate and another type will get a good rate. Mainly, look at this. This
bitter gourd, to which soil does it suit? It suits red soil, black soil, and intermediate soils, which gives a good chance of yield. Many
people say the soil is good, but mainly, those who prepare bitter gourd, for one acre, if we apply cow dung manure up to five to six
trolleys and plow with it at the end, there is a chance of getting a good yield. Definitely, cow dung manure, since the soil's pH level
has already dropped, the pH level... See to it that they use animal manure. It will give them a good chance to get a good yield.
When it comes to seeds, many types of seeds are available in the market. But do you get a big Kakara in the nearby market? Will
the small bitter gourd go away, or will the medium-sized bitter gourd go away? Or, there are two types: white bitter gourd and black
bitter gourd. Choose whichever of these two goes away. It's not about whether they planted it or these people planted it; choose
the bitter gourd that is available in your market. That, whatever seed it is, I will tell you at the very end. In that seed, plant
whichever you like. Mainly, another thing is that many people grow bitter gourd on the ground. When grown on the ground, what
happens is that the yield decreases, and there is a chance of getting more pests. Because the bitter gourd is crooked, they don't
take it in the market because the bitter gourd is crooked. So, if we have quality goods, only then will anyone take it. If you keep it at
one and a half or two feet, we will get good yield air. Because the air circulation is good, there is a chance that mosquitoes, insects,
and pests will be less. When the sunlight falls well on it, the air will be good. There is a chance to withstand the pests a little.
Another thing is, after putting five and a half feet in rows, after putting one and a half feet per plant, what you do is, in the middle,
we tie the logs. I will show you in the video here, look. There, the G wire, the wood, that wood, the G wire is tied to it, and the fish
are getting into it. It goes away at a very low cost. If we tie that net enough, if we put that wire on it, there is a chance to get good
yield. There is also a chance to get the size of the fruit in quality. Those who have permanent pandals, they don't have any
problem. They automatically put it on the pandal, so those who cultivate on the ground, in this trolley system, we have wood in the
middle, G wire to it. Whoever wants to plant bitter gourd, you should definitely use soil, first soil, in the last step, 50 kg of DAP, 50
kg of Potash. For this bitter gourd, potash is very important. 50 kg of DAP, 50 kg of potash, and also 10 kg of seaweed, like Biovita
or Sagarika, are often available. If you take any one of those, it's 10 kg. Next is magnesium sulfate. Magnesium sulfate is important
because the leaves of this bitter gourd tend to turn yellow quickly. Therefore, we should always make sure that the leaves of this
bitter gourd are green. Remember that when this bitter gourd fruit is green, it will be healthy. Another thing is DAP 50 kg, Potash 50
kg, 10 kg of seaweed, and magnesium sulfate. If we mix these four and apply them in the last direction, it will help in getting the
desired quality until the crop is ready. Mainly, after planting these two or three plants, after three or four leaflets, mainly red
beetroot, the worm, comes. So, if you spray medicines like Imlacloprid for that, it will be enough. Next, when this fruit comes, then
the fruit fly comes to it. For that, we have pheromone trips. The size of the fruit, meaning when the fruit is bearing fruit, mainly the
fruit fly comes to it. Then, by putting pheromone trips traps, we can prevent this. Another thing, a down mill will come. When this
comes, mainly if you want to grow in Kakara, if the farmer wants to get out of Kakara, this down mill has to be controlled. If this
comes, then definitely in two or three days at night, there is a chance that the entire crop will be destroyed. Whenever we control
this, then there is a chance for the farmer to get a yield. The main problem in this Kakara is this. Another thing is, if the farmer does
not pay attention to the crop, there is a chance that the entire crop will be lost overnight. That is, whenever the farmer controls
these three things, there is a chance for the farmer to get a yield. Another thing is seed vision. See, seed vision, we have BSF in the
market, BSF company's, Nunas's, Rooshan is there. Also, next is theirs, East West's Pragati 056. Also, next, they have VNR Seeds,
which is Nandita. Also, Syngenta's company has Vallav. Choose any one of these four varieties, or if you want to let the farmers
know that you have planted this seed and it has grown well, please comment. Because even though there are many types of seeds,
we have seen some main ones, so these four types of seeds, whether it is Roshan, Pragati, Nandita, or Vallav, I have mentioned
these four. If you know any seed, please comment.

page_content:
Hello everyone, you are watching Great Maharashtra Channel. So, we always bring you videos of different entrepreneurs—those
who do agriculture-related businesses, like dairy farming, crop farming, and animal husbandry. We bring you videos of many of
them. I am now in Kankavli, which is in the Sindhudurg district, where Nath Pik Farm is located. So, we have visited Sir's farm, and
we will learn about the Pik Farm from him. If you also want to do this business and want to know more about it, their number is on
the screen; you can contact them on that. Please tell me your name and address once. 
Hello. First of all, my greetings to the listeners of Great Maharashtra. My name is Nilesh Sawant, and the name of our pig farm is
Nath Pik Farm, and it is in the Sindhudurg district. It is located in Taluka Kankavli and Bhironde village. How much distance is there
to come here exactly from Kankavli? Taluka Kankavli means it is approximately 11 kilometers away from the district railway station.
And Kolhapur is also near here, right? Kolhapur is 100 kilometers away from here. That means for the people coming from
Maharashtra here, it’s nearby. Yes, they can come via the Mumbai-Goa highway, or they can come via the Pune-Bangalore highway
via Kolhapur. 
Now, how many crops are there on the farm? How many animals are there in total? Sir, we started 3 years ago. On our farm,
including small animals, there are approximately 500 animals now, including kids, etc. So, first introduce yourself—how did you
start this business, and how did you turn to this business? 
Okay. So, as I mentioned, my name is Nilesh Nana Sawant. I am originally from Konkan, from Sindhudurg. You must have seen the
beauty of Konkan. Since childhood, I am actually an engineer. Our initial period was a bit difficult. My father passed away in my
childhood. After that, my mother, by doing small jobs, raised us. I also did part-time college and completed engineering. From the
beginning, I had a desire to come to the village and do something. But at that time, the financial situation was not favorable. I have
worked as an engineer in almost 19-20 countries, in the Middle East, Europe, and Africa. From the beginning, I wanted to work in
our village, in our Konkan region. My mother also wanted it because my father passed away in my childhood. She wanted to live in
the village, as she has always lived in the village. 
Our Konkan is very beautiful; you must have seen it already. So, she and I live here. My son and wife live in Mumbai because their
son is studying. We were together in a foreign country. So, here, it’s me and my mother, and we have some business in Konkan. I
was looking for a business, but in our Konkan region, there aren’t many trees because there isn’t much water, and the land isn’t
good either. Cow rearing and chicken rearing are challenging in today’s context because of labor issues, etc. Since our Goa market
is nearby, we thought that we should do crop farming and pig farming. There is a lot of demand in Goa, and if we do it on dry feed,
then we will definitely have a good demand for it. That’s why we did research for the first one or two years, and we started pig
farming three years ago. 
What difficulties did you face when you were coming to this field? The problem is, generally, when we work in livestock, they get
sick, they die, and their management is hard. It’s not like you just keep them. You have to feed them every day, take care of them,
treat them, check their delivery on time, and monitor their mating. This was very challenging. But one thing about it was that you
couldn’t extract milk, etc. You had to feed them and manage their delivery, which was possible for me because I studied. Since
childhood, I had never touched animals. But I said no, we can do it. 
So, we first researched who is doing this. As I told you, it is done in two ways. One is done on kitchen waste, and the other on dry
feed. We didn’t want to work on kitchen waste because, in kitchen waste, you see, today we are sitting on my farm, and you are
sitting comfortably. With kitchen waste, there is so much foul smell that it is difficult to work. Secondly, the people who work on
kitchen waste are on edge because, look, what does a person need to live? Protein, vitamins, and minerals are needed. But you
don’t get that in kitchen waste; it’s rotten food. It creates toxicity. But when you give dry feed, like corn, soybean, rice DOC, etc., it
has all the proteins, vitamins, and minerals for the body. So, if you do that, the animal grows properly. The animal grows faster, and
the number of offspring is higher. 
From the beginning, we wanted to do dry feed. But there are few people who do that kind of work. We did research. We went to UP,
Haryana, Punjab, and various places to learn how it happens, what kind of animal it is, and how to manage it from the beginning to
the end. 
What did you do after going to Punjab and Haryana? We went there and did research, as you mentioned, like how they perform the
conception of a female, what care should be taken of a pregnant female, what precautions should be taken while delivering, and
what should be done for the newborn. We thoroughly learned what feed to give to the female, how to give it, how the chick grows
from it, in how many months it becomes 100 kg, and how the sale happens. 
So now, in our area, is this business that you are doing the first one, or is it still going on? No, no sir, they are already here. We are
near Goa, so there are many farms here that work on kitchen waste. I think there are only 3-4 farms that work on dry feed. Among
them, our largest farm is in Kankavli, which is in the Sindhudurg district. 
Are you working in the breeding sector? Yes, actually, there is more benefit in working in the breeding sector because if you
increase your own production, it is cheaper for us, meaning the profit is higher. Therefore, breeding is very important. Currently,
how many chicks are there on your farm in total? As I told you earlier, there are a total of 500 animals, small and big, so we have a
mother stock of 50. They have kids with an average of eight. So, approximately, we have 500 animals now. 
How many offspring does a female have in a year, and how is her annual maintenance? Before that, let’s find out when the female’s
mating comes into heat. Generally, when a chick comes out of the mother’s womb, it drinks the mother’s milk for 30-35 days. Then,
after a month, the chick is separated from the mother. In six, seven, or eight months, it becomes 70-80 kg. Once it becomes 70-80

kg, we mate the female, meaning we inseminate it. After insemination, it delivers in about 114 days. During this time, it is
dewormed and given medicines at 30 days, 60 days, and 110 days. 
After delivery, what do you do with her? For one female, approximately, in India, the average is eight offspring. Some females have
16 offspring, some have 14, some have 12, some have 10, some have 7, and some have 6. The average is eight. Our average is
now nine and a half. When we started, our average was eight. 
What new construction have you used in the farm you have developed, and what precautions should be taken while developing the
farm? Actually, the biggest expense in this business is for construction and feed. The female that is breeding needs a space of
50-60 square feet, a separate compartment. After she becomes pregnant, she stays there until delivery. The offspring require 20
square feet of space. For 10 females and 80 offspring, approximately 1,500 square feet of space is needed. 
What feed management do you use, and why should new people do this business only on feed? By doing it on feed, the animal
grows properly. The offspring get all the protein and vitamins, and they grow quickly. There are four types of feed: starter feed,
grower feed, breeder feed, and finisher feed. 
How is its sales market? Selling is easy if you do research and meet people. This business is very good. There are three types of
sales: selling a puppy, selling mutton, and selling females for breeding. 
What method do you use to breed them? It’s a natural method. There is one male for every 10 females. 
What advice would you give to those who want to do this business? Don’t rely on success stories alone. Do your own research. Visit
farms, meet farmers, and ask questions. Understand the budget, shed costs, and feed expenses. Take proper training. 
What services and facilities do you provide for farmers through your farm? We provide complete consultancy, from building the
shed to selling the animals. We help farmers until they come into profit. 
How many entrepreneurs have you created so far? In our area, we have created space for 4 to 5 people. 
Can this business run with laborers, and how to make laborers available? Initially, the owner should prepare to work hard. You can
hire local people or workers from UP, Bihar, or Nepal. 
For the new one, how many animals should they start with initially? It depends on the budget. Start with two females, four females,
or five females, or take 10-50 chicks. 
Where can this business run more? It will work anywhere in Maharashtra. There is a lot of demand for it. 
Is export-import happening in foreign countries as well? Not yet, as the required quality is not being made in our country. 
What advice would you have for those who want to do this business? Do proper research. Don’t go after success stories alone.
Understand the budget and take proper training. 
What is the budget required for one female? You need approximately ₹1,30,000 for one female, including her living space, feed,
and other expenses. 
Congratulations to you from Great Maharashtra Channel for the project you have launched.

your feet will slip. Do not make this mistake even by mistake. Otherwise, you will sit and cry your whole life. Listening to the terrible
consequences of what you have been doing until today will leave you in a great shock. This is a terrible habit of women, selling their
hair for free. But you may not even have a simple idea of how much of a big crisis this one habit can bring on your head. If you hear
how costly this habit is going to be for you, you will give up this obsession forever today.
Recently, a heart-wrenching incident occurred in a corner of Maharashtra. In a small village, a woman selling utensils was
wandering around. She was collecting fallen hair of women and distributing new utensils in exchange. Her bicycle was crowded with
shiny plastic tubs, steel plates, and brightly lit pans. Seeing this sparkling pot, as usual, all the women in the street flocked
together. Taking the old, old, fallen hair stored in the house, all the women gathered there. Giving hair to that pot-maker woman,
each one took their favorite pot into their lap. Someone took a big pot, someone took a pan for the vegetable, while someone
enthusiastically took spoons. But the poor, innocent women didn't know that out of this greed of 2 minutes, they had brought such
a crisis into their lives with their own hands that the whole of Maharashtra was about to be shaken.
In a quiet and serene village in the soil of Maharashtra, a recent event has occurred that has compelled not only that village but the
entire state to think. On one hand, there is the progress of science, and on the other hand, some habits that have been passed
down through tradition. When a mysterious event occurs at the confluence of these two, the ground beneath the feet of a simple,
straightforward person cannot help but slip. That day, it was afternoon. As the sun was scorching, a familiar voice echoed from the
village courtyard. A woman sitting on a bicycle, whose back and both sides of the bicycle were as if laden with mountains of
utensils. A new steel pot, shiny pans, and colorful plastic items. The way to get all this was very easy. Just give the fallen hair from
the house and take this glory home. This is a moment of joy for the women in the village. If the hair that goes into the garbage is
given a new pot, then what is wrong with that? Thinking the same, the women flocked.
In this crowd, there was a woman named Ratna. Ratna is a very hardworking and simple housewife. She also gave the saleswoman
two handfuls of hair that she had been preserving for many days, and in return, she bought a beautiful, bright, sparkling steel pot.
Ratna brought that pot home. In a fit of joy, she washed it clean and cooked vegetables in it for dinner. That night, while eating, a
different experience came. The taste of the vegetable was very different and more delicious than usual. Ratna's husband and
children also ate their fill, but this joy did not last long. It is said that after eating the food cooked in that pot, Ratna's family
suddenly started feeling uneasy. Initially, suspicion was expressed that this was a poisoning from the food, but when the
examination cycle turned, the information that came out was shocking.
When complaints started coming from the houses of not just one person in the village, but also the women who had bought those
utensils, the local administration's ears were cut off. Recognizing the seriousness of this matter, the investigating agencies and
some experts started thinking in this direction. According to the information received from sources, there was not only a simple
vessel behind this incident, but a possibility of a large racket was presented. When the technical study of this case was conducted
on the lines of the CID, some shocking facts came to light. The investigating officers followed up on the question of why these
sellers are giving only discarded hair in exchange for such expensive utensils. You probably don't know, but the hair that we
consider garbage is traded in large quantities in the international market. Scientifically, amino acids are made from human hair,
which is used in some industries. These natural hairs are sold at a rate of ₹5000 to ₹20000 per kilogram. Expensive wigs and hair
extensions are made from these hairs and sold in foreign countries at huge prices.
But the question remains, what exactly happened with the gem? There is a possibility that some harmful chemical elements were
used in the manufacturing process of those utensils or from the metal they were made from. The suspicion that people's health is
not being played with in the pursuit of providing cheap utensils has increased. Our ancestors and religious texts have considered
every part of the body as a specific energy. In scriptures, hair is considered a symbol of Lakshmi. Offering hair at a sacred place like
Tirupati Balaji is considered a sign of dedication and positivity. But when we sell the same hair to a stranger out of greed, we also
give away the positive energy from our home along with it. Such an ideology is prevalent in society. The investigation agencies are
also considering whether this transaction is affecting not only the financial but also the mental and spiritual peace of the people,
